title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that helps developers build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It provides multiple interface options including a standalone desktop app (Antigravity 2.0), IDE integration, CLI, and SDK for flexible agentic workflows.
writeup: This video walks through the four interface options for Google Antigravity, an agent-first software development platform for building, orchestrating, and monitoring AI agents. It compares the standalone desktop app (Antigravity 2.0), IDE integration, the CLI, and the SDK, framing each as a surface for different agentic workflows. The point is matching a surface to a use case: the desktop app and IDE for interactive work, the CLI for command-line use, and the SDK for programmatic integration and code generation.
